title: digera muricata subsp. trinervis
intro: Digera muricata subsp. trinervis, also known as the 'Trinerve Spurge' or 'Wild Bitter Cucumber', is a subspecies of plant belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bitter compounds which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and even acting as a natural remedy for scorpion stings. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
